name: Damage Over Time
description: A status effect that applies damage across multiple ticks after application.
design_notes: Damage over time shifts combat from instant outcomes to pressure management. It should define stacking, refresh, resistance, cleansing, source attribution, and tick timing.
design_purpose: Create lingering threat after an attack lands.
Support poison, burn, bleed, curse, or hazard identities.
Reward sustained pressure and resource management.
player_fantasy: The player feels that a successful application continues to matter after the initial hit.

parameters.name: dot_total_ticks
parameters.description: Number of ticks before the effect expires.

parameters.name: dot_damage_per_tick
parameters.description: Damage applied by each tick before mitigation.

parameters.name: stacking_rule
parameters.description: Rule used when the effect is applied again.

edge_cases: Applying the same DoT from multiple sources needs ownership and stacking rules.
Damage ticks during pause, invulnerability, or death state should be explicit.
Cleansing at the same moment as a tick needs deterministic ordering.
common_bugs: Tick timers drift and deal extra damage over long fights.
Refreshing duration also doubles stack count unintentionally.
Kill credit is lost because the source entity despawned before the final tick.
balancing_notes: High total damage needs counterplay through cleanse, resistance, or avoidance.
Short tick intervals feel responsive but can create UI noise and performance load.
accessibility_notes: Use icons, timers, color-safe overlays, and audio cues to show active harmful statuses.

implementation_notes.unity: Store active DoT effects on a status-effect component with source, remaining ticks, next tick time, and stacking rule; apply damage through the central damage service.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent each DoT as a status resource or dictionary on the actor, tick it from a deterministic timer, and remove it after dot_total_ticks or cleanse events.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement DoT through Gameplay Effects or a replicated status component so periodic damage, source attribution, and removal are server-authoritative.
implementation_notes.web: Keep active status effects in actor state with next_tick_ms and stack metadata, then process ticks in the fixed simulation before rendering floating text or icons.

relationships.reason: Damage-over-time produces repeated health deductions through a ticked damage event stream.
Damage ticks need a reliable interval clock, expiry timestamp, and duplicate-tick prevention.
Rewind systems need explicit history for applied ticks, remaining expiry, and cleansed status effects.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses poison as a visible delayed-damage pressure mechanic.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses burning and poison-like damage over time effects in combat.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses periodic damage effects across combat abilities and statuses.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses poison, burning, and lingering magic effects as visible delayed damage.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses lingering ability damage as visible team-fight pressure.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses poison and toxic states as visible delayed damage pressure.

example_games.title: Diablo III
example_games.note: Uses poison, burning, and other lingering effects as visible combat pressure.
